page_title: Paid family and sick leave — Montana

seo.title: Montana Paid Sick Leave & Family Leave 2026 · Teamed
seo.meta_description: Montana has no state paid sick leave mandate and no state PFML. Federal FMLA at 50 employees. Here's what employers must know — and how to design a competitive leave programme.
seo.og_title: Montana paid leave: no state mandate, but the WDEA makes policy drafting critical
seo.og_description: No state sick leave law. No PFML programme. Federal FMLA at 50 employees. And the WDEA turns every word of your offer letter into a legal document. Here's what Montana employers need.

hero.alt: Montana big sky panorama — rolling sage-brush plains beneath a wide open sky with distant Rocky Mountain silhouettes and pine-covered ridgelines.

statutory_facts.claim: Montana has no state-mandated paid sick leave for private employers
Montana has no state paid family and medical leave programme
statutory_facts.statute: No Montana PFML statute enacted as of May 2026

statutory_facts.claim: State employees accrue sick leave from day 1; paid out after 90 days continuous service
Federal FMLA applies at 50 employees within 75 miles; 12 weeks unpaid
Montana requires reasonable unpaid leave for pregnancy (anti-discrimination basis)
WDEA makes written employer policies (incl. leave terms) legally binding
Montana minimum wage 2026: $10.85/hour; no tip credit
Accrued vacation treated as earned wages where employer policy creates reasonable expectation of payment
statutory_facts.statute: Montana case law / WDEA wage fringe-benefits construct
statutory_facts.note: General Montana legal principle from compliance cache; specific case citations should be verified with Montana employment counsel before publish

The underlying principle is well-established but exact precedents not cited.
